elements.title: printing press
elements.contents: ... that around 1473 a Dominican friar denounced the printing press (example pictured) as a "whore" set against the "virgin" pen?

elements.title: an Australian newspaper
elements.contents: ... that an Australian newspaper is mainly written by people who are or have been in prison?

elements.title: who was murdered at the age of 27
elements.contents: ... that an endowed scholarship was established at Thomas Jefferson University in memory of transgender activist and fashion designer Dominique "Rem'mie" Fells, who was murdered at the age of 27?

elements.title: Notharctus
elements.contents: ... that Notharctus, one of the first fossil primates discovered in the Americas, was originally classified as a pachyderm?

elements.title: Daz Black
elements.contents: ... that many people attributed Andrew Tate's removal from social media to a diatribe by Daz Black?

elements.title: ethnic Turks were forcibly renamed
elements.contents: ... that around 850,000 ethnic Turks were forcibly renamed by the People's Republic of Bulgaria in the 1980s?

elements.title: Patricio Fontanet
elements.contents: ... that Patricio Fontanet, Eduardo Vázquez, and other neighbourhood friends gave their first show as Callejeros in front of a barbershop?

elements.title: Roman Gardens
elements.contents: ... that Rudolph Valentino was rumored to have used Roman Gardens for secret romances, even though he died before the building was completed?

elements.title: Steve Baumgartner
elements.contents: ... that Steve Baumgartner had more than 1,200 beer cans lining the walls of a spare bedroom in his house?
